title: Logs, Events, Metrics, Inspect, Debugging, and Incident Evidence · Delivery Case: Docker Events Inspect State
summary: A service restarts intermittently with no useful application error while disk, memory, network, and health evidence disagree. Planned artifact: Add and verify the docker events inspect state increment in layered Docker incident evidence packet. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Correlate time-bounded events with inspect state, exit cause, OOM, restart count, health output, mounts, networks, and effective configuration.
Design stdout and stderr logging plus driver, delivery, rotation, retention, metadata, backpressure, and secret-redaction policy.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# docker-basics cumulative container delivery portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic manifest and evidence review only.
# They never connect to a Docker daemon, mount a socket, or execute learner commands.
workspace: docker
version-baseline:
  engine: 29.6.1
  compose: 5.3.1
  buildx: 0.35.0
  buildkit: 0.31.1
  dockerfile-frontend: 1.25
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-environment
evidence:

steps.title: Predict Docker Events Inspect State · Theory Docker Events Inspect State
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory container exits only after revision 2; an operator restarts it and edits files with exec before collecting state. Reconstruct the first causal layer from inspect, events, logs, stats, and release identity. Competency probe: Correlate time-bounded events with inspect state, exit cause, OOM, restart count, health output, mounts, networks, and effective configuration.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Name client, daemon, builder, image, container, host-kernel, storage, network, registry, and supply-chain boundaries; predict state and authority; review the smallest deterministic manifest change; then verify a failure, repair, cleanup, and live-environment transfer limit.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: A worked example container exits only after revision 2; an operator restarts it and edits files with exec before collecting state. Reconstruct the first causal layer from inspect, events, logs, stats, and release identity. Competency probe: Correlate time-bounded events with inspect state, exit cause, OOM, restart count, health output, mounts, networks, and effective configuration. A defensible solution applies Docker Events Inspect State, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to layered Docker incident evidence packet.

content.code:
# Evidence: docker-worked-docker-observability-debugging-5
  - competency: docker-worked-docker-observability-debugging-5
    artifact: incident-evidence.yaml
    decision: worked docker observability debugging 5
    scenario-variant: 4
    environment: ephemeral development host with constrained disk and memory
    fault-injection: rebuild after the approved base digest is withdrawn
    retained-proof: accessible recovery runbook with rollback rehearsal timing
    case-key: worked5
    change: |
      baseline: ps-inspect-events-logs-stats
      correlation: release-digest-and-container-id
      mutation-policy: reproduce-before-exec
      diagnostic-image: separately-reviewed
      conclusion: first-causal-layer
    verify: docker inspect failed-api --format json && docker logs --timestamps failed-api
    failure: Reject interactive mutation as diagnosis, unbounded log collection, high-cardinality labels, or a restart presented as root-cause evidence.
    repair: Preserve state and timestamps, compare desired with observed configuration, isolate one layer, reproduce cleanly, and retain the causal regression.
content.caption: Runnable Docker artifact and transfer-evidence contract example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: The latest container log line is sufficient to identify every Docker lifecycle cause. This fails the Docker Events Inspect State contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.

content.label: Mastery evidence
content.value: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Correlate time-bounded events with inspect state, exit cause, OOM, restart count, health output, mounts, networks, and effective configuration.

# Evidence: docker-events-inspect-state-1x2oyq
  - competency: docker-events-inspect-state
    artifact: incident-evidence.yaml
    decision: events inspect state
    scenario-variant: 1
    environment: rootless remote builder on an arm64 staging host
    fault-injection: invalidate one cache input without changing application source
    retained-proof: timestamped lifecycle transition and bounded cleanup result
    case-key: 1x2oyq
    change: |
      baseline: ps-inspect-events-logs-stats
      correlation: release-digest-and-container-id
      mutation-policy: reproduce-before-exec
      diagnostic-image: separately-reviewed
      conclusion: first-causal-layer
    verify: docker inspect failed-api --format json && docker logs --timestamps failed-api
